symptoms.symptom_name: Sudden severe continuous tearing right-upper-quadrant pain

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Hepatocellular carcinoma with intratumoural haemorrhage / capsular rupture
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Acute focal capsular pain in a cirrhotic suggests bleeding into or stretch/rupture of a hepatic tumour.
RUQ pain is compatible but does not explain the massive irregular hepatomegaly and tense ascites.

symptoms.symptom_name: Rapidly worsening abdominal distension with orthopnoea (cannot lie flat) and breathlessness

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Rapid fluid accumulation splinting the diaphragm produces orthopnoea and tachypnoea; refractory to the patient's diuretics.
A surge in ascites over 24 hours signals a decompensation event (portal-vein thrombosis, tumour expansion).
New jaundice marks worsening hepatic synthetic/excretory failure and supports decompensated liver disease.

symptoms.symptom_name: Four months of unintentional weight loss, anorexia and fatigue

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Constitutional cachexia over months is a classic malignancy prodrome in a cirrhotic at risk of HCC.
A racing heart and weakness may be the only patient-reportable proxies for the sealed tachycardia and borderline blood pressure.
Cirrhosis from chronic HCV is the dominant risk factor for HCC and frames the whole presentation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Hard, irregular, tender hepatomegaly 6 cm below the costal margin with a hepatic bruit

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A hard irregular hepatic mass with a bruit (hypervascularity) is highly specific for HCC — a sealed clinician finding not obtainable via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Tense ascites confirmed by strongly positive shifting dullness

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Shifting dullness confirming tense ascites is a clinician bedside finding; the patient can only report the visible distension.

symptoms.symptom_name: Tachycardia (HR 115), borderline hypotension (BP 100/60), delayed capillary refill (3s)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective haemodynamic warning signs — sealed clinician measurements; the AI infers circulatory concern from the patient's reported racing heart and weakness, not a measured number.

symptoms.symptom_name: Alpha-fetoprotein (AFP) and multiphase contrast CT / diagnostic paracentesis

investigation_details.investigation_name: AFP + multiphase contrast-enhanced CT liver + diagnostic paracentesis
investigation_details.expected_result: Elevated AFP; hepatic mass with arterial enhancement and washout; ascitic fluid analysis for malignant cells / SBP
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: The HCC and the cause of the acute decompensation cannot be confirmed or excluded — these are hospital investigations, never available in telehealth in real time.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Confirmatory imaging/markers define the diagnosis and are the reason the correct telehealth action is escalation, not diagnosis.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Acute decompensation of HCC in cirrhosis
symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Acutely decompensated hepatocellular carcinoma with suspected haemorrhage / tense ascites
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Focal tearing pain + surging tense ascites + new jaundice + racing heart/weakness in a known HCV cirrhotic is a time-critical emergency pattern that the AI must recognise and escalate.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Emergency department assessment with multiphase contrast CT, AFP, FBC/coagulation and diagnostic paracentesis
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Defines the mass, checks for haemorrhage, and excludes SBP — the AI arranges this via emergency escalation but cannot perform any of it.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: AI Doctor ordering or interpreting bloods/imaging itself
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Breath-Ezy cannot draw bloods, run AFP, image the liver or perform paracentesis; attempting to 'order and await results' would dangerously delay escalation.
